The Enron Corporation, once the largest energy trader in the world, is now in bankruptcy. But the company's many hands are still shaping the American economy. 

The company's chief executive, Kenneth L. Lay, is a former Enron chairman and chief executive, and its chief financial officer, Andrew S. Fastow, was once the head of Enron's trading business. 

The company's trading business, which was once the envy of Wall Street, collapsed in the fall of 2000, and the company's stock has fallen to a fraction of its former value. But Enron still has a huge presence in the energy market, and its trading business is still the largest in the United States.

o Net Works Names New President and CEO

NEW YORK -- Net Works, a provider of Internet
services, said it named John Viverito as president and
CEO. Mr. Viverito will report to the company's
new chairman and chief executive officer, John
McAfee. Mr. McAfee will continue to serve as
chairman and chief executive officer.

Mr. Viverito most recently served as president and
CEO of Net Works' parent, Enron Net Works. Prior to
joining Enron, Mr. Viverito was president and
CEO of Internet Solutions, a provider of
network services and software for the Internet.

"John brings a wealth of experience and a proven track record
in the Internet industry," said Mr. McAfee. "He has
been a key player in the development of the
Internet industry and we are excited to have him join our
team."

Mr. Viverito will report to the company's board of directors.

Trading on the index is done on the NYSE floor.  If you are trading on the NYSE floor, you will need to have a NYSE broker account.  If you do not have a NYSE broker account, you will need to open one before you can trade on the index.  

If you have any questions, please call the NYSE Customer Service Center at (212) 299-7000.
